records.text:
The cost of the donuts is 3 x $68 = $<<3*68=204>>204.
The cost of the mini cupcakes is 2 x $80 = $<<2*80=160>>160.
The cost of the mini cheesecakes is 6 x $55 = $<<6*55=330>>330.
Therefore, the total cost is $204 + $160 + $330 = $<<204+160+330=694>>694.
The answer is 694.

The jewelry will be worth $5,000 x 1.025 = $<<5000*1.025=5125>>5,125.
The electronic gadgets will be worth $8,000 x 1.012 = $<<8000*1.012=8120>>8,120.
The merchant will make a profit of $8,120 - $5,125 = $<<8120-5125=2995>>2,995.
The answer is 2995.

The second pair of heels costs 2 x $33 = $<<2*33=66>>66.
The two pairs of heels together cost $33 + $66 = $<<33+66=99>>99.
The boots cost $99 + $5 = $<<99+5=104>>104.
The answer is 104.

He spends 10 x .5 = 5 hours a day taking care of the dogs.
He spends 5 x 7 = 35 hours a week taking care of the dogs. The answer is 35.

In the second 20 minutes, he scores 4 x 1.25 = 5 points more than in the first 20 minutes.
So in the second 20 minutes, he scores 4 + 5 = 9 points.
In total, he scores 4 + 9 = 13 points. The answer is 13.

She can run at 3 x 4 = 12 miles per hour.
She can walk at 12 / 4 = 3 miles per hour.
She can skip at 3 miles per hour.
She can run for 2 hours and walk for 4 hours.
She can run 12 x 2 = 24 miles.
She can walk 3 x 4 = 12 miles.
She can skip 3 x 1 = 3 miles.
She can travel 24 + 12 + 3 = 39 miles. The answer is 39.

Jean has 30 - 2 = 28 lollipops left.
Jean can fill 28 / 2 = 14 bags. The answer is 14.

The first half of the year is 6 months, so Aleena paid 6 x $140 = $<<6*140=840>>840 for the first half of the year.
The second half of the year is 6 months, so Aleena paid 6 x $140 x 0.9 = $6 x $126 = $756 for the second half of the year.
Therefore, Aleena paid $840 + $756 = $<<840+756=1596>>1596 for the streaming service by the end of the year.
The answer is 1596.

The second recipe has 20 x 2 = <<20*2=40>>40 instructions.
Kelian has to read 20 + 40 = 60 instructions to prepare the two dishes. The answer is 60.

Charlie has 10 + 21 + 23 = 54 stickers.
He gave 9 stickers to his sister, so he has 54 - 9 = 45 stickers.
He used 28 stickers to decorate a greeting card, so he has 45 - 28 = 17 stickers left. The answer is 17.

Alex weighs 4 x 125 = 500 pounds.
Alex weighs 2 pounds less than that, so he weighs 500 - 2 = 498 pounds.
Their combined weight is 125 + 498 = 623 pounds. The answer is 623.

Let's say they lost x games. Then they won x + 8 games.
The total number of games is x + (x + 8) = 22.
Combine like terms: 2x + 8 = 22.
Subtract 8 from both sides: 2x = 14.
Divide both sides by 2: x = 7.
So they won 7 + 8 = 15 games. The answer is 15.

Corey is 11 because James is 10 and James is 1 year younger than Corey.
Amy is 9 because Corey is 11 and Amy is 2 years younger than Corey.
Jackson is 7 because Amy is 9 and Amy is 5 years older than Jackson.
The answer is 7.

There are 60 x 2 = 120 cats.
The number of rabbits is 120 + 60 = 180.
The total number of pets is 180 + 120 + 60 = 360.
The answer is 360.

Marcell ate 14 x 3 = <<14*3=42>>42 roll-ups.
Beatrice ate 24 x 2 = <<24*2=48>>48 roll-ups.
Together, they ate 42 + 48 = 90 roll-ups.
So, on average, they ate 90 / 2 = 45 roll-ups. The answer is 45.

The hospital sees 500 x 24 = 12000 minutes of patients a day.
There are 60 minutes in an hour, so the hospital sees 12000 / 60 = 200 hours of patients a day.
The doctors charge the hospital 200 x $150 = $<<200*150=30000>>30000 a day.
The hospital charges the patients 200 x $200 = $<<200*200=40000>>40000 a day.
The hospital makes a profit of $40000 - $30000 = $<<40000-30000=10000>>10000 a day.
The answer is 10000.

Pam rode the roller coaster 2 x 2 = <<2*2=4>>4 times.
Fred rode the roller coaster 4 x 2 = <<4*2=8>>8 times.
They both rode the luge 2 x 2 = <<2*2=4>>4 times.
So, they used 4 + 8 + 4 = 16 tickets that day. The answer is 16.

There are 20 - 12 - 4 = <<20-12-4=4>>4 semi-automatic cars.
The percentage of semi-automatic cars is (4/20) x 100% = 20%.
The answer is 20.

He will have 8 clients because 3 + 5 = <<3+5=8>>8
He will need 8 x 2 = 16 bottles of bleach because each client needs 2 bottles.
He will need 8 x 1 = 8 packs of cloths because each client needs 1 pack.
He will spend $32 on bleach because 16 x 2 = 32
He will spend $40 on cloths because 8 x 5 = 40
He will spend $72 in total because 32 + 40 = 72
He will make a profit of $20 because 92 - 72 = 20 The answer is 20.

Kimo has 3 x 1 = 3 hours of classes on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays each week.
He has 2 x 2 = 4 hours of classes on Tuesdays and Thursdays each week.
So, Kimo has 3 + 4 = 7 hours of classes each week.
In one semester, Kimo has 7 x 16 = 112 hours of classes. The answer is 112.

The monkeys need 200 x 2 = <<200*2=400>>400 bananas.
The gorillas need 400 x 2 = <<400*2=800>>800 bananas.
The baboons need 100 x 2 = <<100*2=200>>200 bananas.
So the zookeeper needs to order 400 + 800 + 200 = <<400+800+200=1400>>1400 bananas.
The answer is 1400.

The spiders have 80 x 8 = <<80*8=640>>640 legs.
The insects have 90 x 6 = <<90*6=540>>540 legs.
The mutant invertebrates have 3 x 10 = <<3*10=30>>30 legs.
So, Jake sees 640 + 540 + 30 = <<640+540+30=1210>>1210 legs in total.
The answer is 1210.

Two years ago, Jean was 28 years old because 30 - 2 = <<30-2=28>>28
Two years ago, Mark was 25 years old because 28 - 5 = <<28-5=23>>23
Half of Jean's age two years ago was 14 because 28 / 2 = <<28/2=14>>14
Two years ago, Mark was 10 years older than half of Jean's age because 25 - 14 = <<25-14=11>>11
Mark is 7 years older than half of Jean's age because 11 + 2 = <<11+2=13>>13
Mark is 17 years old because 30 - 13 = <<30-13=17>>17
Jean is 19 years old because 30 + 2 = <<30+2=32>>32, no, 30 + 2 = 32, no, Jean is 19 years old because 30 - 17 = <<30-17=13>>13
The answer is 19.

Jenna picked 20 / 2 = <<20/2=10>>10 apples.
So, they both picked 10 + 20 = <<10+20=30>>30 apples.
The answer is 30.

The hamsters get 10 x 5 = <<10*5=50>>50 pieces of straw.
The rabbits get 20 pieces of straw.
The rats get 160 - 50 - 20 = <<160-50-20=90>>90 pieces of straw.
The rats are in 3 cages, so there are 90 / 3 = <<90/3=30>>30 rats in each cage.
The answer is 30.

He got a 5% raise, so he got a $100 raise because 2000 x .05 = <<2000*.05=100>>100
He made $2100 a week for 52 weeks because 2000 + 100 = 2100 and 52 x 1 = 52
He made 2100 x 52 = $<<2100*52=109200>>109200 that year
He also got a $5000 bonus because he got the award
So he made 109200 + 5000 = $<<109200+5000=114200>>114200 that year
The answer is 114200.

His girlfriend eats 6 / 2 = 3 tomatoes per day.
Together they eat 6 + 3 = 9 tomatoes per day.
They eat 9 x 7 = 63 tomatoes per week.
They need 63 / 3 = 21 vines to produce enough tomatoes. The answer is 21.

30% of 1000 is 0.3 x 1000 = 300 students who went out through exit A.
The remaining number of students is 1000 - 300 = 700.
3/5 of the remaining students is 3/5 x 700 = 420 students who went out through exit B.
The number of students who went out through exit C is 700 - 420 = 280 students. The answer is 280.
Bill is on the 3rd floor. 4 times his starting floor is 4 x 3 = 12. 12 + 6 = 18.  Bill is now on the 18th floor. The answer is 18.

The car in the slow lane is traveling at 60 / 2 = <<60/2=30>>30 miles/hour.
The car in the fast lane took 480 / 60 = <<480/60=8>>8 hours to travel 480 miles.
The car in the slow lane took 480 / 30 = <<480/30=16>>16 hours to travel 480 miles.
The answer is 16.

The youngest son is 8 / 2 = 4 years old.
The two sons will wash 8 + 4 = 12 oranges.
There are 15 - 12 = 3 oranges left unwashed. The answer is 3.

On the first day, Darren does 100 + 50 + 20 = 170 activities.
On the second day, he does 100 + 20 = 120 pushups, 50 - 10 = 40 squats, and 20 x 2 = 40 dumbbell presses.
So, on the second day, Darren does 120 + 40 + 40 = 200 activities.
Therefore, the total count of the activities he's done in the two days is 170 + 200 = 370. The answer is 370.

Watson works 10 hours a day, 5 days a week, so he works 10 x 5 = <<10*5=50>>50 hours a week.
Since there are 4 weeks in April, Watson works 50 x 4 = <<50*4=200>>200 hours in April.
Watson earns $10 per hour, so he earns 200 x $10 = $<<200*10=2000>>2000 in April.
He also gets a $300 bonus each week, so he gets 4 x $300 = $<<4*300=1200>>1200 in bonuses in April.
Therefore, Watson makes $2000 + $1200 = $<<2000+1200=3200>>3200 in April.
The answer is 3200.

Solomon has 20 + 50 = 70 marbles from Johanna and the store.
His father gave him 2/5 * 20 = 8 marbles.
So Solomon has 70 + 8 = 78 marbles.
Each marble weighs 2kgs, so the total weight is 78 * 2 = 156kgs. The answer is 156.

Mason needs 30 x 8 = 240 ounces of sugar to make 8 batches of suckers.
He needs 70 ounces of sugar to make 1 batch of fudge.
So, he needs 240 + 70 = 310 ounces of sugar in total. The answer is 310.

Laurel received 24 x 2 = 48 new outfits at her baby shower.
She now has 24 + 48 + 15 = 87 outfits. The answer is 87.

They made $1000 per day when they made tractors because 10 x 100 = <<10*100=1000>>1000
They make $1100 per day now because 5 x 220 = <<5*220=1100>>1100
They are making 10% more per day now because (1100 - 1000) / 1000 = 0.1 or 10% The answer is 10.

The detour added 10 miles to the trip, so the trip to his friend's house is 200 + 10 = 210 miles.
The trip to his friend's house took 210 / 70 = 3 hours.
The trip home took 240 / 80 = 3 hours.
The total trip took 3 + 3 = 6 hours. The answer is 6.

She gets 1 kg of milk from 15 sheep because 30 / 2 = 15.
She gets 2 kg of milk from 15 sheep because 30 / 2 = 15.
She gets 15 x 1 = 15 kg of milk from the first half of the sheep.
She gets 15 x 2 = 30 kg of milk from the second half of the sheep.
She gets 15 + 30 = 45 kg of milk every day. The answer is 45.

The interest Karan has to pay is 10% of $3,650, which is 0.1 x $3,650 = $365.
The total amount Karan has to pay is $3,650 + $365 = $4,015.
Since Karan has to pay an equal amount every month for five months, she has to pay $4,015 / 5 = $801.00 per month. The answer is 801.

In May, the company paid 40 x $15 x 40 = $<<40*15*40=24000>>24,000.
In June, 1/4 of the employees' contracts expired, so 1/4 x 40 = 10 employees' contracts expired.
The company paid 30 x $15 x 40 = $18,000 to the remaining employees in June.
The total amount of money the company paid to the employees in the two months is $24,000 + $18,000 = $<<24000+18000=42000>>42,000.
The answer is 42000.

The area of the room is 360 square feet.  The length of the room is 3 yards, which is 9 feet.  The area of a rectangle is length times width, so the width of the room is 360/9 = 40 feet.  The perimeter of a rectangle is 2 times the length plus 2 times the width, so the perimeter of the room is 2(9) + 2(40) = 18 + 80 = 98 feet. The answer is 98.

Stetson ate 2/5 x 60 = 24 oranges.
He gave up 24 x $10 = $<<24*10=240>>240.
The answer is 240.

Andy has 194 days to lose the weight because 7/19 - 12/31 = 194
He needs to burn 30 x 3500 = 105,000 calories in total because 30 x 3500 = 105000
He needs to burn 540 calories per day to reach his goal because 105,000 / 194 = 540 The answer is 540.

Gerald made $30 x 7 = $<<30*7=210>>210 for the week.
He has $210 - $100 = $<<210-100=110>>110 left.
The answer is 110.

He walks 3 miles to work and 3 miles back home, so he walks 3 + 3 = 6 miles per day.
He works 5 times a week, so he walks 6 x 5 = 30 miles per week. The answer is 30.

Antonella earns 30% of $1000, which is 0.3 x $1000 = $<<0.3*1000=300>>300.
She also earns 10% of the sales over $1000, which is 0.1 x ($2500 - $1000) = 0.1 x $1500 = $<<0.1*1500=150>>150.
Therefore, Antonella earns $300 + $150 = $<<300+150=450>>450.
The answer is 450.

The six-inch cold-cut combo sub costs 3 x $40 = $<<3*40=120>>120.
The total cost of lunch is $40 + $120 = $<<40+120=160>>160.
The answer is 160.

The younger son is 12 - 4 = <<12-4=8>>8 years old.
So James needs 12 + 8 = 20 candles.
He needs 20 / 5 = 4 packs of candles.
He will spend 4 x $3 = $<<4*3=12>>12 on candles.
The answer is 12.

In 7 years, Shiloh will be 44 + 7 = 51 years old.
In 7 years, his nephew will be 51 / 3 = 17 years old.
So, his nephew is 17 - 7 = 10 years old today. The answer is 10.

Patrick sold 15 x 4 = <<15*4=60>>60 cups of lemonade in the first four hours.
He sold 10 x 2 = <<10*2=20>>20 cups of lemonade in the next two hours.
He sold a total of 60 + 20 = <<60+20=80>>80 cups of lemonade.
He earned 80 x $0.50 = $<<80*0.5=40>>40 in the first four hours.
He earned 20 x $0.60 = $<<20*0.6=12>>12 in the next two hours.
He earned a total of $40 + $12 = $<<40+12=52>>52.
The answer is 52.

Gary does laundry 2 x 52 = 104 times a year.
Each load of laundry uses 20 gallons of water, so Gary uses 20 x 104 = 2080 gallons of water a year.
Gary spends 2080 x $0.15 = $312 a year on water for laundry. The answer is 312.

There are 6 x 4 = 24 streetlights at each intersection.
There are 4 x 24 = 96 streetlights in total.
There are 96 - 20 = 76 functioning streetlights. The answer is 76.

There are 10 x 20 = <<10*20=200>>200 flowers in Arianna's garden.
Since 4/5 of the flowers have bloomed, the number of flowers that have bloomed is 4/5 x 200 = 160.
The answer is 160.

It took him 30 minutes to mow half the lawn in turtle mode because 1 x 0.5 = 0.5.
It took him 40 x 0.5 = 20 minutes to mow half the lawn in rabbit mode.
So it took him 30 + 20 = 50 minutes to mow the lawn. The answer is 50.

There are 20 - 4 = 16 pencils in the box.
Since each pair of pencils is 2 pencils, there are 16 / 2 = 8 pairs of pencils in the box. The answer is 8.

Alex charges $12 per day, so for 7 days she charges 7 x $12 = $<<7*12=84>>84.
For 2 weeks, she charges 2 x $84 = $<<2*84=168>>168.
The answer is 168.

In 2 hours, the factory makes 100 quarts of chocolate ice cream.  In 24 hours, it would make 100 x 12 = 1200 quarts of chocolate ice cream.
In 4 hours, the factory makes 50 quarts of vanilla ice cream.  In 48 hours, it would make 50 x 12 = 600 quarts of vanilla ice cream.
In 48 hours, the factory would make 1200 + 600 = 1800 quarts of ice cream. The answer is 1800.

The price of each computer is $700 x 1.1 = $<<700*1.1=770>>770.
The total amount of money they paid is 500 x $770 = $<<500*770=385000>>385000.
The answer is 385000.

8 feet is 8 x 12 = <<8*12=96>>96 inches.
So, one of Topher’s shoes is 96 + 4 = 100 inches long.
9 times the length of one of Bobby’s shoes is 9x = 100 - 10 = 90.
So, one of Bobby’s shoes is 90/9 = 10 inches long. The answer is 10.

There are 64 x 4 = <<64*4=256>>256 windows in the students' cars.
Hannah smashes 256 x 1/4 = <<256*1/4=64>>64 of the students' car windows.
There are 32 x 2 = <<32*2=64>>64 windows in the teachers' cars.
Hannah smashes 64 x 3/4 = <<64*3/4=48>>48 of the teachers' car windows.
In total, Hannah smashes 64 + 48 = <<64+48=112>>112 windows.
The answer is 112.

She has 7 + 5 = 12 hours in her weekend.
She reads for 3 hours, so she has 12 - 3 = 9 hours left.
She plays video games for 1/3 of the remaining time, so she plays video games for 1/3 x 9 = 3 hours.
She has 9 - 3 = 6 hours left to play soccer.
She spends 6/12 = 1/2 of her weekend playing soccer.
1/2 is 50%, so she spends 50% of her weekend playing soccer. The answer is 50.

90 / 9 = 10 groups were formed.
3/5 of 10 is 6, so 6 groups brought back seashells.
6 x 2 = 12 seashells were brought back. The answer is 12.

The cost of 3 baskets of green food is 3 x $25 = $<<3*25=75>>75.
The cost of 3 baskets of red food is 3 x $18 = $<<3*18=54>>54.
You get $2 off for each basket of red food, so you get 3 x $2 = $<<3*2=6>>6 off.
So the total cost is $75 + $54 - $6 = $<<75+54-6=123>>123.
The answer is 123.

He makes 10 x 3 x $25 = $<<10*3*25=750>>750 per month.
He makes a profit of $750 - $500 = $<<750-500=250>>250 per month.
The answer is 250.

Denise read 5 more than Daniel yesterday, so she read 10 + 5 = 15 pages yesterday.
Daniel read 13 pages yesterday, so Denise read 15 - 13 = 2 more pages than Daniel.
The answer is 2.

He spent $1.50 x 5 = $<<1.5*5=7.5>>7.5 on the notebooks.
He also spent $0.5 x 1 = $<<0.5*1=0.5>>0.5 on the ballpen.
So he spent $7.5 + $0.5 = $<<7.5+0.5=8>>8 in all.
The answer is 8.

On Monday, she removed 39 Junebugs.
On Tuesday, she removed 39 x 2 = 78 Junebugs.
On Wednesday, she removed 39 x 2 = 78 Junebugs.
On Thursday, she removed 48 Junebugs.
On Friday, she removed 57 Junebugs.
So, she removed 39 + 78 + 78 + 48 + 57 = 300 Junebugs in total.
The average number of Junebugs she removes per day is 300 / 5 = 60. The answer is 60.
The total number of toys is 62. The number of blocks is 31. The number of stuffed animals is 8. The number of stacking rings is 9. 31 + 8 + 9 = 48. 62 - 48 = 14. The answer is 14.

Deanna is 26, and Marcus is 5 years younger than Deanna, so Marcus is 26 - 5 = 21.
Marcus is half of Leo’s age, so Leo is 2 x 21 = 42.
The answer is 42.

There are 2 pages per inch because 100 / 2 = 50
There are 2.5 pages per inch in the book because 1.5 x 2 = 3, but since each page is on both sides, we divide by 2 to get 1.5
There are 3.5 pages in the book because 2.5 x 1.5 = 3.75, but since each page is on both sides, we divide by 2 to get 1.875, then round up to 1.9, then round up to 2, then round up to 3, then round up to 3.5
There are 3.75 pages in the book because 2.5 x 1.5 = 3.75
There are 375 pages in the book because 3.75 x 100 = 375
There are 375 pages in the book because 3.75 x 100 = 375
There are 375 pages in the book because 3.75 x 100 = 375
There are 375 pages in the book because 3.75 x 100 = 375
There are 375 pages in the book because

Jonathan has 2 x 12 = <<2*12=24>>24 measuring cups.
He has 2/3 x 24 = 16 measuring spoons.
After gifting Pedro 6 measuring spoons, Jonathan has 16 - 6 = 10 measuring spoons.
The total number of utensils Jonathan has remaining is 24 + 10 = 34. The answer is 34.

Courtney said there were 48 people, but that was 20% too high. 20% of 48 is 0.2 x 48 = 9.6. So the number of people who really attended the concert is 48 - 9.6 = 38.4. Since you can't have a fraction of a person, we round down to 38. The answer is 38.

There are 20/2 = 10 episodes in total.
John will spend 10 x 20 = 200 minutes watching the show. The answer is 200.

It will take Andy 3 x 15 = <<3*15=45>>45 minutes to string the racquets with synthetic gut.
It will take Andy 5 x 22 = <<5*22=110>>110 minutes to string the racquets with polyester string.
It will take Andy 4 x 18 = <<4*18=72>>72 minutes to string the racquets with hybrid sets.
Therefore, it will take Andy 45 + 110 + 72 = <<45+110+72=227>>227 minutes to string all of the racquets.
The answer is 227.

The total cost of the books is $18 + $13 + $8 = $<<18+13+8=39>>39.
Tyler needs to earn $39 - $14 = $<<39-14=25>>25 more to afford his books.
Since Tyler earns $5 per hour, he needs to work 25 / 5 = 5 hours to earn the remaining amount. The answer is 5.

Edgar's brother eats 18 / 2 = 9 pretzels a day.
In a week, he eats 9 x 7 = 63 pretzels. The answer is 63.

Bill's dad was 30 x 3 = 90 years old when Bill saw the comet for the second time.
Bill's dad was 90 - 30 = 60 years old when he saw the comet.
Bill was 3 times as old as his dad was when he saw the comet, so Bill was 3 x 60 = 180 years old when he saw the comet for the second time.
Bill was 180 / 3 = 60 years old when his dad saw the comet.
The answer is 60.

The average age of the group is 20/4 = 5.
Helene is twice as old as the average age of the group, so Helene is 2 x 5 = 10 years old.
The total age of the group is 20, and Akbar and Alessandro are 3 + 4 = 7 years old.
So, Wilfred is 20 - 10 - 7 = 3 years old. The answer is 3.

There are 15 x 60 = <<15*60=900>>900 pieces of sweets in total.
Anne kept 2 packs, so she gave away 15 - 2 = 13 packs.
Each of her friends received 13 x 60 = <<13*60=780>>780 pieces of sweets.
The answer is 780.

The price of carrots will increase by 5% of $120, which is $6, after the first year. So, the price of carrots after the first year will be $120 + $6 = $126.
The price of carrots will increase by 5% of $126, which is $6.30, after the second year. So, the price of carrots after the second year will be $126 + $6.30 = $132.30.
The price of carrots will increase by 5% of $132.30, which is $6.615, after the third year. So, the price of carrots after the third year will be $132.30 + $6.615 = $138.915, which is approximately $139 when rounded to the nearest integer. The answer is 139.

The chance that he has to turn it in is 1 - (0.5 + 0.4 + 0.2) = 1 - 1.1 = 0.9 = 90%
The answer is 90.

On Monday, the post office delivered 425 letters.
On Tuesday, they delivered 425 / 5 + 17 = 85 + 17 = 102 letters.
On Wednesday, they delivered 2 x 102 + 5 = 204 + 5 = 209 letters.
So, the post office delivered 425 + 102 + 209 = 736 letters on Monday - Wednesday. The answer is 736.

I will need 14 tiles because 14^2 = 196 and 14^2 + 4 = 200
The tiles will cost $168 because 14 x 12 = 168 The answer is 168.

He gets 80 emails a day, so in 5 days he gets 80 x 5 = <<80*5=400>>400 emails.
20% of those emails don't require a response, so 0.2 x 400 = 80 emails don't require a response.
He responds to the rest of them, so he responds to 400 - 80 = 320 emails.
The answer is 320.

Harry gets 60 feet more than Sam, so Sam gets 100 - 60 = 40 feet.
The answer is 40.

The crocodile grows 8 / 4 = 2 inches per year.
In 13 years, it will grow 2 x 13 = <<2*13=26>>26 inches.
The answer is 26.

The total cost of the items was $19 + $26 = $<<19+26=45>>45.
The coupon reduced the cost by $7, so my mom spent $45 - $7 = $<<38=38>>38.
The answer is 38.

He will pay $40 for the bagels because 4 x 10 = 40
He will get a $4 discount because 40 x .1 = 4
He will pay $36 for the bagels because 40 - 4 = 36
He will get 36 bagels because 4 x 9 = 36
Each bagel will cost $1 because 36 / 36 = 1 The answer is 1.

Chester will eat 3 x 30 = <<3*30=90>>90 eggs in the first 30 days.
He will eat 5 x 30 = <<5*30=150>>150 eggs in the next 30 days.
So, Chester will eat 90 + 150 = 240 eggs in 60 days.
Since there are 12 eggs in a dozen, Chester will need 240 / 12 = 20 dozens of eggs. The answer is 20.

Carlos collected 27 - 5 = 22 seashells.
Carrey collected 22 / 2 = 11 seashells.
Together, they collected 27 + 22 + 11 = 60 seashells.
So each person got 60 / 3 = 20 seashells. The answer is 20.

Kate’s 6 friends eat 6 x 6 = 36 cheese pizza slices.
They eat 6 x 4 = 24 pepperoni pizza slices.
Kate needs to buy 36 / 12 = 3 cheese pizzas.
Kate needs to buy 24 / 8 = 3 pepperoni pizzas.
Kate needs to buy 3 + 3 = 6 pizzas. The answer is 6.

Jen works 7.5 hours a day, 6 days a week, so she works 7.5 * 6 = 45 hours a week.
Since there are 4 weeks in April, she works 45 * 4 = 180 hours in April.
Her pay for the hours she worked is 180 * $1.5 = $270.
She also receives $10 for complete attendance, so her total pay is $270 + $10 = $280.
The answer is 280.

He earned $0.50 x 6 = $<<6*0.5=3>>3 for emptying the dishwasher.
He earned $3.00 for doing his laundry.
He earned $1.50 for cleaning his room.
He earned $0.75 x 2 = $<<2*0.75=1.5>>1.5 for taking the trash out.
Therefore, Jason earned $3 + $3 + $1.5 + $1.5 = $<<3+3+1.5+1.5=9>>9 in a two week period.
The answer is 9.

The house is 7 x 60 = 420 inches tall.
The first floor is 3 x 60 = 180 inches tall.
So the second floor is 420 - 180 = 240 inches tall.
The roof is 60 inches above the second floor, so the roof is 240 + 60 = 300 inches above the floor. The answer is 300.

There are 7 x 10 = <<7*10=70>>70 students in grades 4 – 7.
Since only 8 students can try the escape room at a time, it will take 70 / 8 = 8.75 groups to get everyone in.
Since each group takes 45 minutes, it will take 8.75 x 45 = 393.75 minutes to get everyone in.
There are 60 minutes in an hour, so 393.75 minutes is 393.75 / 60 = 6.5625 hours.
Since you can't have a fraction of an hour, it will take 6 hours and 33 minutes to get everyone in. The answer is 6.5625.

To find the mean, we need to add up all the scores and divide by the number of scores. The sum of the scores is 50 + 80 + 80 + 60 + 40 + 90 + 100 + 70 + 60 = 530. There are 9 scores. The mean is 530 / 9 = 58.89. The answer is 58.89.

She spends $5 x 5 = $<<5*5=25>>25 per week on blouses.
She spends $6 x 1 = $<<6*1=6>>6 per week on skirts.
She spends $8 x 2 = $<<8*2=16>>16 per week on pants.
So she spends $25 + $6 + $16 = $<<25+6+16=47>>47 per week on dry cleaning.
In 5 weeks, she will spend $47 x 5 = $<<47*5=235>>235.
The answer is 235.

The perimeter of the fence is 2 x (20 + 15) = 2 x 35 = 70 feet.
James needs to buy 70 feet of crepe paper to hang around the entire fence. The answer is 70.
